message:
chore: audit batch 392 (19 files, ~6115L, 0 bugs) + rustfmt replay.rs

Fresh-eyes audit of recently added FABRIC and test files:
- capability/routing.rs (796L), capability/tokens.rs (718L), compiler.rs (147L), explain.rs (89L)
- fabric_lab_integration.rs (937L), progressive_disclosure_contract.rs (164L), adversarial_stress.rs (131L)
- replay_e2e_suite.rs (1433L), semantic_golden_fixture.rs (714L), scenario_adapter.rs (431L)
- 4 lab differential/normalization/taxonomy contract tests (786L)
- 6 WASM/websocket contract tests (1112L)
- tests/common/{mod,e2e_harness}.rs (1001L)
All SOUND, 0 bugs found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.2","title":"[FABRIC][P14.2] CRDT propagation and convergence","description":"Build the propagation and convergence mechanism for CRDT surfaces. File: src/messaging/control.rs (propagation portion)

- Optimistic propagation across stewards and relays
- Leaderless convergence on cold paths and during transient partitions
- Delta compression for efficient propagation
- Anti-entropy protocol for convergence verification
- Dramatically cheaper high-fanout consumer churn (no sequencer bottleneck for interest changes)
- Much lower pressure on authoritative sequencer lease
- CRDTs complement RaptorQ data capsules: authoritative control stays fenced, bulk payloads stay recoverable, mergeable summaries stop central metadata from becoming bottleneck
- Tests: propagation across multiple nodes, convergence after partition, delta compression efficiency, anti-entropy correctness","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:28:41.702104821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:01.397588793Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crdt","fabric","propagation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:28:41.702104821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:01.397534171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.3","title":"[FABRIC][P14.3] Unit tests for CRDT control surfaces","description":"Comprehensive tests for the CRDT control surfaces.

- Each CRDT type: merge, delta, convergence, bounded error
- Propagation: multi-node, partition, reconnection, delta compression
- Integration: CRDT + control capsule (verify CRDTs never absorb authoritative state)
- Concurrency: concurrent merges, out-of-order deltas
- Lab runtime: deterministic CRDT convergence
- Performance: benchmark merge and delta operations
- Detailed logging","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:28:47.284373546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:10.524905619Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crdt","fabric","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:28:47.284373546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:10.419522074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.15.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:10.524841310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","title":"[FABRIC][P15] Advanced Semantic Features","description":"# Phase 15: Advanced Semantic Features

## Purpose
Build the advanced features that make the fabric a new category of distributed systems substrate: evidence-native data plane, cut-certified mobility, workflow/saga kernel, counterfactual control, distributed supervision, bounded-regret reliability, consistency certificates, recoverable service capsules, intent-compiled operations, etc.

## Features
1. Evidence-Native Data Plane: FrankenSuite evidence for routing/delivery decisions
2. Cut-Certified Mobility: hot-cell evacuation with proof no obligations orphaned
3. Subject-Native Workflow/Saga Kernel: durable workflow steps as subject transitions + obligations
4. Continuous Counterfactual Control: shadow branches from certified cuts, compare candidate worlds
5. Distributed Supervision Compiler: supervision graphs + subject morphisms + failure domains
6. Bounded-Regret Reliability Control: adaptive tuning within declared safety envelopes
7. Local-to-Global Consistency Certificates: sheaf/obstruction-based topology checking
8. Recoverable Service Capsules: portable hosted state with authority-scrubbing on restore
9. Intent-Compiled Operations: operator intent surface compiled to policy artifacts
10. Commutativity-Aware Sharding: semantic independence partitioning above canonical cell ownership
11. Branch-Addressable Reality: live/lagged/replayed/canary/forensic branch attachment
12. Causality-Native Streams: partial-order views above linear control kernels
13. Budget-Carrying Traffic: overload decisions driven by semantic damage not just load
14. Certified Cut Lattice & Reality Index: policy-scoped retained index of meaningful cuts
15. Privacy-Budgeted Metadata Surfaces: differential-privacy noise on exported metadata
16. Quantitative Obligation Contracts: SLO-style probabilistic contracts above binary obligations
17. Reactive Protocol Synthesis: generate handler skeletons from protocol contracts
18. Speculative Subject-Cell Execution: tentative publish with deterministic rollback

Each of these is a separate task within this epic.

## FABRIC North Star Reference
This phase is governed by the five cross-cutting FABRIC success criteria defined in `src/messaging/mod.rs` and `docs/FABRIC_GUARDRAILS.md`:
1. Keep the public mental model NATS-small.
2. Make stronger guarantees explicit named service classes, never hidden taxes on the common case.
3. Lower radical behavior into small, inspectable artifacts.
4. Keep autonomous policy loops inside declared safety envelopes with replay, evidence, and rollback.
5. Target the first product wedge at systems that need sovereignty, durable partial progress, and post-incident explanation together.

If work in this phase violates those criteria, or drifts toward the anti-goal of a grand unified fabric that makes the common case slower and less legible than NATS, it must be redesigned.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:03:30.064771820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:58.317505983Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:03:30.064771820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:16:06.828016150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:15:31.495403568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.1","title":"[FABRIC][P15.1] Evidence-native data plane","description":"Attach FrankenSuite evidence to important routing and delivery decisions. File: src/messaging/explain.rs

- Important messaging/routing choices attached to evidence entries and decision contracts
- Not for every hot-path packet — for transitions that matter operationally
- Evidence for: security-sensitive routing, adaptive delivery policy, multi-tenant governance, distributed failover, operator trust
- System answers 'what happened?' AND 'what policy decided it, with what evidence?'
- Integration with franken_evidence and franken_decision
- Tests: evidence attachment for each decision type, query evidence by decision, evidence retention policy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:52.297010831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:52.297010831Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","evidence","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:52.297010831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Not for every hot-path packet — for transitions that matter operationally
- Evidence for: security-sensitive routing, adaptive delivery policy, multi-tenant governance, distributed failover, operator trust
- System answers 'what happened?' AND 'what policy decided it, with what evidence?'
- Integration with franken_evidence and franken_decision
- Tests: evidence attachment for each decision type, query evidence by decision, evidence retention policy","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackMeadow","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:52.297010831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T20:04:39.012600548Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","evidence","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:29:52.297010831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.10","title":"[FABRIC][P15.10] Commutativity-aware sharding and scheduling","description":"Partition and schedule by semantic independence above canonical cell ownership. File: src/messaging/fabric.rs (sharding portion)

- Protocol-carrying subjects, declared interference classes, and obligation footprints make it explicit which transitions commute
- Secondary execution-lane partitioning by semantic independence (not just subject name)
- Non-interfering conversation families routed to separate lanes without unnecessary coordination
- High-contention namespaces decomposed by declared shared-state footprint (not crude subject prefixes)
- Protocol kernels advertise when reordering or parallel issuance is lawful
- Hot paths get throughput by proving independence (not hand-tuned partitioning)
- Keeps canonical hash-stable SubjectCell ownership as base boundary, adds semantic concurrency above
- Tests: independence detection, lane partitioning, throughput improvement with independent conversations","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:31:43.737440119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:31:43.737440119Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","sharding"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.10","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:31:43.737440119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.11","title":"[FABRIC][P15.11] Branch-addressable reality","description":"Make multiple realities addressable within the hosted boundary. File: src/messaging/cut.rs (branch addressing portion)

- Live branch, lagged certified cut, replayed branch with different policy, canary branch (fenced from production), forensic branch
- Non-operator client workflows default to read-only observation or explicitly sandboxed mutation lanes
- Only works where external side effects fenced off or modeled explicitly
- Safer rollouts (canaries from realistic state), better support (reproduce user-visible outcomes against real semantic cut), branch-based policy evaluation without synthetic fixtures, 'explain this response' workflows
- BranchAddress struct: branch_type (Live, Lagged, Replayed, Canary, Forensic), cut_id, policy_override, access_policy
- Tests: attach to each branch type, read-only enforcement for non-operator, canary fencing, forensic explanation query","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:31:58.941338672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:34:07.051296996Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","branch","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.11","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:31:58.941338672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.11","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:34:07.051225022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.11","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:34:06.836702257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.11","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:34:06.944936612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.12","title":"[FABRIC][P15.12] Causality-native streams and views","description":"Expose causal structure and bounded linearization policies. File: src/messaging/stream.rs (causality portion)

- For selected workloads: preserve causal structure, let consumers choose linearization policy
- NOT replacing per-cell append sequencing or control-capsule linearity
- Selected higher-level views derived from dependency-tracked events and bounded frontier summaries
- Consumers subscribe to causally complete cut or dependency-certified frontier (not arbitrary merged total order)
- Multi-source workflows merge by dependency (not arrival order)
- Better incident explanation: concurrency ambiguity explicit, not collapsed into one log order
- Only valuable when paired with interference-aware scheduling (P15.10)
- Tests: causal dependency tracking, frontier computation, linearization policy selection, multi-source merge","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:32:17.530224355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:34:07.157196914Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","causality","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.12","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:32:17.530224355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.12","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:34:07.157117186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -4782,8 +4782,8 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.5","title":"[FABRIC][P5.5] Unit tests for streams","description":"Comprehensive tests for the stream subsystem. Stream lifecycle: create, configure, publish, consume, close. Two-phase publish: normal, cancel, concurrent, ack timing. Retention: all three policies with edge cases. Capture filtering: matching and non-matching subjects. Dedupe: within window, outside window, edge cases. Mirror/source: child region creation, quiescence, failover. Snapshots: create, restore, fork, replay. Cancel-correctness: cancel at every phase transition. Lab runtime: deterministic scheduling of stream operations. Detailed structured logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:00.017798459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.740281351Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fabric","streams","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:00.017798459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.407264638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.519498141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.629612406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.740203767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","title":"[FABRIC][P6] Consumers as Policy-Driven Delivery Engines","description":"# Phase 6: Consumers as Policy-Driven Delivery Engines

## Purpose
Build fabric-hosted consumers as region-owned delivery engines backed by obligations, not just counters. NATS consumer policy is richer than most realize — preserve all of that richness and make it even stronger with decision contracts.

## Core Design
- Consumers are region-owned delivery engines
- Ack state backed by obligations, not only counters
- Delivery attempts carry explicit attempt identity and stale-ack idempotence rules
- Pull requests, flow control, pause windows, heartbeats, and scheduling policies are explicit policy objects
- Delivery selection incorporates budgets, backlog, ack debt, and fairness policy

## Decision-Contract-Driven Scheduling
The deepest NATS-inspired move: treat consumers as scheduling entities
- Pull-consumer priority groups → typed demand classes
- Pull-consumer pinned-client policy → temporary capability leasing
- Pull-consumer overflow policy → bounded fallback rule with evidence
- Redelivery policy → decision contract recording why retry/delay/dead-letter was chosen

## Adaptive Consumer Kernel
- Stable deterministic default policies
- Optional advanced policies that remain audit-backed and replayable
- No hand-wavy exactly-once claims
- Very strong reasoning about pending work and retry causality

## Consumer Modes
- Push mode: messages pushed to subscriber with backpressure
- Pull mode: consumer pulls specific sequence windows or demand classes
- Priority groups within pull consumers
- Pinned-client delivery
- Overflow and prioritized policies

## Relation to Existing Code
- combinator/circuit_breaker.rs has similar adaptive policy patterns
- service/retry.rs has retry policy concepts
- obligation/ledger.rs tracks ack obligations
- franken_decision has decision contract infrastructure

## FABRIC North Star Reference
This phase is governed by the five cross-cutting FABRIC success criteria defined in `src/messaging/mod.rs` and `docs/FABRIC_GUARDRAILS.md`:
1. Keep the public mental model NATS-small.
2. Make stronger guarantees explicit named service classes, never hidden taxes on the common case.
3. Lower radical behavior into small, inspectable artifacts.
4. Keep autonomous policy loops inside declared safety envelopes with replay, evidence, and rollback.
5. Target the first product wedge at systems that need sovereignty, durable partial progress, and post-incident explanation together.

If work in this phase violates those criteria, or drifts toward the anti-goal of a grand unified fabric that makes the common case slower and less legible than NATS, it must be redesigned.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:58:28.074079521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:29.965164645Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["consumers","delivery","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:58:28.074079521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:06:09.722429795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.1","title":"[FABRIC][P6.1] Consumer policy types and delivery engine","description":"Build the consumer delivery engine with policy-driven scheduling. File: src/messaging/consumer.rs. Consumer struct: region-owned, carries ConsumerConfig, ConsumerState, DeliveryPolicy. ConsumerConfig: durable_name, filter_subject, ack_policy, max_deliver, max_ack_pending, max_waiting, ack_wait, replay_policy, deliver_policy, flow_control, heartbeat, idle_heartbeat. ConsumerState: delivered_count, pending_count, ack_floor, pending_acks (backed by obligations). DeliveryMode enum: Push (messages pushed with backpressure), Pull (consumer requests specific windows). PullRequest struct: batch_size, demand_class, max_bytes, expires, no_wait. FlowControl: explicit heartbeats, max_waiting, pause/unpause. Tests: consumer creation, mode switching, pull requests, flow control.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TurquoiseTower","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:09.130657674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T19:38:34.461652458Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T19:38:34.461630938Z","close_reason":"Implemented consumer delivery-engine policy types, pull/push scheduling, flow-control gating, ack bookkeeping, and focused consumer unit coverage in src/messaging/consumer.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["consumers","fabric","policy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:09.130657674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2528,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"NATS implementation reference: server/consumer.go reveals consumers are really mini scheduling kernels, not just 'cursor + ack'. Key features to preserve: push/pull modes, flow control, heartbeats, max waiting, max ack pending, pause/unpause, pull-consumer priority groups, pull-consumer pinned-client delivery, pull-consumer overflow and prioritized policies. The Asupersync version makes these even stronger: priority groups become typed demand classes, pinned-client becomes temporary capability leasing, overflow becomes bounded fallback with evidence, redelivery becomes decision contract. This creates an 'adaptive consumer kernel' with stable deterministic defaults plus optional advanced policies that are audit-backed and replayable.","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:49:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","title":"[FABRIC][P6.2] Obligation-backed ack state","description":"Back consumer ack state with obligations instead of just counters. File: src/messaging/consumer.rs. Each delivery attempt creates a delivery obligation in the ledger. Ack commits the obligation, Nack aborts it. Delivery attempts carry explicit attempt identity and stale-ack idempotence rules. AttemptCertificate struct: cell_id, epoch, cursor_authority_lease, sequence_or_window, delivery_attempt, obligation_id. Redelivery creates new obligation with reference to previous attempt. Dead-letter: obligation transferred to DLQ with reason record. Tests: ack/nack obligation lifecycle, stale ack detection, redelivery with new obligation, dead-letter transfer.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TurquoiseTower","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:29.981909012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T19:38:49.123705260Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ack","consumers","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:29.981909012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.845780381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","title":"[FABRIC][P6.3] Decision-contract-driven consumer scheduling","description":"Make consumers decision-contract-driven schedulers using FrankenSuite. File: src/messaging/consumer.rs. Pull-consumer priority groups as typed demand classes. Pull-consumer pinned-client policy as temporary capability leasing. Pull-consumer overflow policy as bounded fallback rule with evidence. Redelivery policy as decision contract recording why retry/delay/dead-letter was chosen. ConsumerDecisionContract: record every material delivery decision with evidence. Adaptive consumer kernel: stable defaults + optional advanced policies (audit-backed, replayable). Integration with franken_decision infrastructure. Tests: priority group delivery, pinned-client capability lease, overflow with evidence, redelivery decision contracts, adaptive policy switching.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:43.349384325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:56.188054673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["consumers","decision-contract","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:43.349384325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.958423605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:06.066758033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2537,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Adaptive consumer kernel: (1) STABLE deterministic defaults, (2) OPTIONAL advanced audit-backed replayable policies, (3) NO exactly-once claims (idempotency+leases), (4) STRONG pending-work reasoning via obligation-backed acks, (5) RETRY CAUSALITY via decision contracts explaining WHY each redelivery happened.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:10:39Z"},{"id":2540,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Adaptive consumer kernel concept (from proposal): The adaptive consumer kernel has these properties: (1) STABLE defaults: deterministic, well-understood delivery policies that require no configuration. (2) OPTIONAL advanced policies: operator can opt into smarter delivery scheduling, but these are always audit-backed and replayable. (3) NO exactly-once claims: the system is honest about at-least-once delivery with idempotency + leases at boundaries. (4) STRONG reasoning about pending work: because ack state is backed by obligations, the system can always answer 'what is still owed?' precisely. (5) RETRY CAUSALITY: because redelivery decisions are captured in decision contracts, the system can explain the full chain of why a message was redelivered N times and what happened at each attempt. This last point is the key differentiator: most systems can tell you a message was redelivered 5 times, but Asupersync can tell you WHY each redelivery happened, what the competing considerations were, and what evidence justified the decision.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:56Z"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","title":"[FABRIC][P6.2] Obligation-backed ack state","description":"Back consumer ack state with obligations instead of just counters. File: src/messaging/consumer.rs. Each delivery attempt creates a delivery obligation in the ledger. Ack commits the obligation, Nack aborts it. Delivery attempts carry explicit attempt identity and stale-ack idempotence rules. AttemptCertificate struct: cell_id, epoch, cursor_authority_lease, sequence_or_window, delivery_attempt, obligation_id. Redelivery creates new obligation with reference to previous attempt. Dead-letter: obligation transferred to DLQ with reason record. Tests: ack/nack obligation lifecycle, stale ack detection, redelivery with new obligation, dead-letter transfer.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TurquoiseTower","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:29.981909012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T20:04:13.560053215Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T20:04:13.560033769Z","close_reason":"Implemented obligation-backed consumer ack state in src/messaging/consumer.rs: ledger-backed delivery obligations, ack commit / nack abort, redelivery supersession, dead-letter transfer, owner/stats surfaces, and focused fabric_consumer_* coverage validated with rch cargo check plus 10 passing consumer tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ack","consumers","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:29.981909012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.845780381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","title":"[FABRIC][P6.3] Decision-contract-driven consumer scheduling","description":"Make consumers decision-contract-driven schedulers using FrankenSuite. File: src/messaging/consumer.rs. Pull-consumer priority groups as typed demand classes. Pull-consumer pinned-client policy as temporary capability leasing. Pull-consumer overflow policy as bounded fallback rule with evidence. Redelivery policy as decision contract recording why retry/delay/dead-letter was chosen. ConsumerDecisionContract: record every material delivery decision with evidence. Adaptive consumer kernel: stable defaults + optional advanced policies (audit-backed, replayable). Integration with franken_decision infrastructure. Tests: priority group delivery, pinned-client capability lease, overflow with evidence, redelivery decision contracts, adaptive policy switching.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TurquoiseTower","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:43.349384325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T20:04:23.126041364Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["consumers","decision-contract","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:43.349384325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:05.958423605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:06.066758033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2537,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Adaptive consumer kernel: (1) STABLE deterministic defaults, (2) OPTIONAL advanced audit-backed replayable policies, (3) NO exactly-once claims (idempotency+leases), (4) STRONG pending-work reasoning via obligation-backed acks, (5) RETRY CAUSALITY via decision contracts explaining WHY each redelivery happened.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:10:39Z"},{"id":2540,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Adaptive consumer kernel concept (from proposal): The adaptive consumer kernel has these properties: (1) STABLE defaults: deterministic, well-understood delivery policies that require no configuration. (2) OPTIONAL advanced policies: operator can opt into smarter delivery scheduling, but these are always audit-backed and replayable. (3) NO exactly-once claims: the system is honest about at-least-once delivery with idempotency + leases at boundaries. (4) STRONG reasoning about pending work: because ack state is backed by obligations, the system can always answer 'what is still owed?' precisely. (5) RETRY CAUSALITY: because redelivery decisions are captured in decision contracts, the system can explain the full chain of why a message was redelivered N times and what happened at each attempt. This last point is the key differentiator: most systems can tell you a message was redelivered 5 times, but Asupersync can tell you WHY each redelivery happened, what the competing considerations were, and what evidence justified the decision.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.4","title":"[FABRIC][P6.4] Unit tests for consumers","description":"Comprehensive tests for the consumer delivery engine. Consumer lifecycle: create, configure, deliver, ack, close. Push mode: backpressure, flow control, heartbeats. Pull mode: batch requests, demand classes, max_waiting. Obligation-backed acks: create, commit, abort, stale detection. Decision contracts: redelivery decisions, priority groups, overflow. Cancel-correctness: cancel during delivery, cancel during ack. Lab runtime: deterministic scheduling. Performance: benchmark delivery path. Detailed structured logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:52.981747304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:06.390192633Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["consumers","fabric","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:23:52.981747304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:06.171166758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:06.281415262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:24:06.390130167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.8","title":"[FABRIC][P7] Control Plane as First-Class Subjects","description":"# Phase 7: Control Plane as First-Class Subjects

## Purpose
Model the control plane as first-class subjects in the same fabric, not a separate imperative API. NATS's $SYS.* pattern is powerful — extend it with region-owned handlers, FrankenSuite evidence, and capability domains.

## Control Subject Families
- Fabric health
- Import/export changes
- Route and gateway advisories
- Lameduck and drain state
- Auth and capability failures
- Consumer pause and unpin events
- Stream snapshot creation and restore
- Repair, replay, and rebalance advisories

## Design Guardrails
- System/control subjects live in dedicated capability domain
- Control handlers run with reserved budget and priority (not competing with user traffic)
- Minimal bootstrap and break-glass recovery path available even when ordinary fabric is degraded
- Advisory subjects do NOT automatically feed policy loops without explicit damping/stratification/operator intent

## Asupersync-Unique Additions
1. Control-plane handlers and advisories are region-owned and replayable (once external inputs modeled)
2. Material decisions emit FrankenSuite evidence and decision records

Operators see more than "gateway detached" — they see:
- Which capability graph edges were affected
- Which obligations were transferred, aborted, or replay-scheduled
- Which policy decided the failover path
- What evidence justified it

## Relation to Existing Code
- grpc/health.rs has health check infrastructure
- server/shutdown.rs has graceful shutdown with mark_stopped wakeup
- signal/graceful.rs has graceful signal handling
- franken_evidence and franken_decision provide evidence/decision machinery

## FABRIC North Star Reference
This phase is governed by the five cross-cutting FABRIC success criteria defined in `src/messaging/mod.rs` and `docs/FABRIC_GUARDRAILS.md`:
1. Keep the public mental model NATS-small.
2. Make stronger guarantees explicit named service classes, never hidden taxes on the common case.
3. Lower radical behavior into small, inspectable artifacts.
4. Keep autonomous policy loops inside declared safety envelopes with replay, evidence, and rollback.
5. Target the first product wedge at systems that need sovereignty, durable partial progress, and post-incident explanation together.

If work in this phase violates those criteria, or drifts toward the anti-goal of a grand unified fabric that makes the common case slower and less legible than NATS, it must be redesigned.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:58:44.838338372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:33.063968201Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advisories","control-plane","fabric"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:58:44.838338372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:06:43.996557063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:07:19.517838253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.8.1","title":"[FABRIC][P7.1] System subject namespace and control handlers","description":"Build the system subject namespace modeled after NATS $SYS.*. File: src/messaging/control.rs

- System subject families: $SYS.FABRIC.HEALTH.*, $SYS.FABRIC.IMPORT.*, $SYS.FABRIC.EXPORT.*, $SYS.FABRIC.ROUTE.*, $SYS.FABRIC.DRAIN.*, $SYS.FABRIC.AUTH.*, $SYS.FABRIC.CONSUMER.*, $SYS.FABRIC.STREAM.*, $SYS.FABRIC.REPAIR.*, $SYS.FABRIC.REPLAY.*
- System/control subjects live in dedicated capability domain (require AdminControl capability)
- Control handlers run with reserved budget and priority (NOT competing blindly with user traffic)
- Minimal bootstrap and break-glass recovery path available even when ordinary fabric is degraded
- Advisory subjects do NOT automatically feed policy loops without explicit damping/stratification/operator intent (prevent control plane amplifying its own observations)
- ControlHandler struct: region-owned, reserved budget, priority scheduling
- Tests: system subject registration, capability domain enforcement, reserved budget allocation, break-glass path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:51.474889422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T18:36:50.691075966Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T18:36:50.690975549Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["control-plane","fabric","system"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.8.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:51.474889422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2529,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.8.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"NATS implementation reference: server/events.go shows the control-as-subjects pattern: $SYS.ACCOUNT.*.CONNECT, $SYS.ACCOUNT.*.DISCONNECT, $SYS.SERVER.*.LAMEDUCK, $SYS.SERVER.*.SHUTDOWN, $SYS.SERVER.*.CLIENT.AUTH.ERR, $SYS.LATENCY.*, $SYS.REQ.*. This is powerful because the system describes itself in its own native coordination medium. Asupersync goes further: (1) control handlers are region-owned and replayable, (2) material decisions emit FrankenSuite evidence records. CRITICAL GUARDRAIL: advisory subjects must NOT automatically feed policy loops without explicit damping, stratification, or operator intent — otherwise the control plane will amplify its own observations.","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:49:02Z"}]}
